editionData.title: Daily Brief for April 25, 2026: Global Headlines, Markets, Hacker News, Product Hunt
editionData.description: A daily brief for April 25, 2026 covering global headlines, market closes, Hacker News, and Product Hunt.
editionData.author: Sony Mathew

headlines.title: Iran War Live Updates: Witkoff and Kushner to Go to Pakistan for Talks on Iran
headlines.summary: The two American envoys are expected to leave Saturday for Pakistan. Iran’s foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi, arrived in Islamabad on Friday.

headlines.title: Gaza Set to Hold First Local Election in Two Decades
headlines.summary: Hamas said it was not participating in the municipal elections in Deir al-Balah, set for this weekend. Residents said it was a long-awaited opportunity to address the city’s problems

headlines.title: Europeans Ask: What Would It Mean to Defend One Another Without NATO?
headlines.summary: European Union nations have a little-known obligation to protect one another. Experts caution it is no replacement for NATO.

headlines.title: In Britain, 7 Unelected Lords Help Block an Assisted Dying Bill
headlines.summary: A small group in the House of Lords proposed hundreds of amendments that helped stymie a bill that was meant to legalize medically assisted death for the terminally ill.

headlines.title: ‘Didi vs. Modi’: A Test for the Hindu Right in India’s Bengali Heartland
headlines.summary: The party of Prime Minister Narendra Modi has been making gains in a state once beyond its grasp, but many decry an audit that removed nine million voters, many of them Muslim.

headlines.title: Iran war: What’s happening on day 57 as US envoys head to Pakistan?
‘Cautious optimism’: Iran FM in Islamabad; US says envoys on way for talks
Police raid Peru’s election authorities after outcry over slow vote count
Israel kills at least 12 Palestinians in Gaza amid ‘ceasefire’
US sending envoys to Pakistan, raising hopes of talks with Iran’s Araghchi
Justice Department to allow firing squads for executions in move to ramp up capital punishment
headlines.summary: The Justice Department will adopt firing squad as a permitted method of execution as the Trump administration moves to ramp up and expedite capital punishment cases.

headlines.title: Appeals court rules that Trump's asylum ban at the border is illegal
headlines.summary: A U.S. appeals court ruled Friday that immigration laws allow people to apply for asylum at the border, and the president cannot bypass this. The decision stems from Trump declaring the border situation an invasion and suspending asylum.

headlines.title: From night life in Egypt to rice farming in Vietnam, the war in Iran is a drain
headlines.summary: Fuel costs more. Food is harder to get. Jobs are evaporating. And in Cairo, cafes and restaurants are ordered to close at 9 p.m.

headlines.title: A real-life Kraken stalked the seas of the late Cretaceous
headlines.summary: Researchers discovered evidence of enormous Kraken-like creatures who hunted in the seas some 100 million years ago, competing with large apex predators.

headlines.title: Justice Department drops probe into Fed Chair Jerome Powell
headlines.summary: The move paves the way for the Senate to confirm Kevin Warsh, the president's nominee to head the central bank.

headlines.title: EU leaders to address Iran war fallout, mutual defense at Cyprus summit
headlines.summary: AYIA NAPA, Cyprus - European Union (EU) leaders gathering in Cyprus on Thursday and Friday will discuss the economic and security fallout from Iran war, especially on soaring energy prices and mutual defense following a recent drone attack on the Mediterranean island.

headlines.title: SE. Asia seeks domestic fuels amid energy crunch
headlines.summary: Southeast Asian countries are leaning harder on homegrown energy sources — from coal to palm oil and sugarcane — as soaring costs and supply risks make Middle Eastern oil and gas imports less viable.

headlines.title: Red Cross envoy applauds Beijing's humanitarian action
headlines.summary: Balthasar Staehelin, personal envoy of the International Committee of the Red Cross president to China, has lauded China's increasing engagement in international humanitarian action, praising the country's consistent support for respect for international humanitarian law and its diplomatic efforts to promote the peaceful resolution of crises.

headlines.title: Mutual gains via Five-Year Plan welcomed
headlines.summary: China and the US can create a better future for relations, Chinese Ambassador to the United States Xie Feng said on Monday, calling on the nations to seize the opportunities presented by China's 15th Five-Year Plan (2026-30).

headlines.title: Economies to feel war heat across globe
headlines.summary: The war in the Middle East will likely affect the global economy and the United States, whether it continues for a short or long term, because of disruptions in the oil market that could trigger inflation and spur a recession, warn experts, economists and the International Monetary Fund.

headlines.title: Raghav Chadha, 6 AAP MPs join BJP
headlines.summary: Raghav Chadha and six AAP MPs resign, merging with BJP, citing party's deviation from core values and interests.

headlines.title: Congress leader’s son shot dead, another son injured in Janjgir-Champa in Chhattisgarh
headlines.summary: Gunmen kill a Congress leader's son and injure another in Chhattisgarh home invasion; police investigate the motive.

headlines.title: 10 of 26 petitioners caught in Russia-Ukraine crossfire dead; some volunteered, others duped, Govt tells SC
headlines.summary: Ten of 26 petitioners, claimed to be human-trafficked to Russia, are dead amid ongoing legal and governmental challenges.

headlines.title: Economist Ashok Lahiri, scientist Gobardhan Das from Bengal to join Niti Aayog as vice-chairman, member
headlines.summary: Ashok Lahiri and Gobardhan Das appointed to Niti Aayog as vice-chairman and member, enhancing the think tank's expertise.

headlines.title: BJP staring at electoral losses in West Bengal, Tamil Naidu, Kerala: Farooq Abdullah
headlines.summary: Farooq Abdullah predicts BJP electoral losses in West Bengal, Tamil Nadu, and Kerala, citing a rejection of divisive politics.

headlines.title: Exclusive: US State Dept orders global warning about alleged AI thefts by DeepSeek, other Chinese firms
Iran to make offer aimed at satisfying US demands, Trump says
King Charles to attend 9/11 event with New York Mayor Mamdani
US judge dismisses Musk's fraud claims in OpenAI case at his request, plans to proceed to trial
Witkoff and Kushner headed to Pakistan for Iran talks, White House says
